reviews.review_description: when playing on the Virtual pool   and shooters pool on the PC  you push on  (M) button to move around  with the mouse  and The (S) button for the zoom ...when Developers agree to use a options like that in Pool  and Snooker..then and only then these games will bee good and probably more popular

reviews.review_title: Has Potential, but for now sux
reviews.review_description: Playing against the bots, the second and fourth bot was HUGE.  Took up a lot of area, blocking the table so that I could not shoot if the cue ball or others were on the side of the table it was standing. It was all body, the width of the table and so tall its head was through the ceiling of the room.  The body also extended over the table, so it blocked your view of some of the balls making it extremely hard to see and hit them.  A few times it said it was a "foul" when it was not.  After these glitches are fixed and with a few other improvements, this could be a really fun game.
i love game

Probably the best game on vr. The fact that it’s free makes it such a hidden gem. Although there is one feature that if it would be added, it would really be the best game. And that is different types of spin shots. Other than that, fabulous game.

Could be a good ham but it is way too glitchy. I only played five minutes an I fell through the floor to infinity and the second AI player I played took up th entire screen and I couldn’t see the table. Too bad, could be fun.
Almost  realistic.. Must try

So it’s a good game in general but there are a few problems plus some shady sh*t. Firstly, the second and fourth person you play in single player are so bugged that there is this huge 3D character moving around while you’re playing blocking the view of your shots, takes some finesse but you can beat them. Then with it saying it’s free is total BS. After you beat the fourth person or a time limit of playing it wants you to pay $5 a month just to play this, I of course am not doing that and hit the big red X and it restarts to the main menu where I of course hit single player again and the same thing keeps popping up. I would have bought the game but it wants $5 a month just to play this. I would advise finding another pool game if they want to make this a subscription base just to play this.
This game is absolutely cheeks i got the game a year ago and i still had to pay for the subscription today even though i got it when it was free and didn't have to pay. this game is absolutely buns.

So I don’t know who is on the development team but they obviously don’t play pool or snooker! 
First off, not sure what’s going on with the AI but was given a foul for hitting my own ball, but that’s not what’s wrong with this game. The cueing system sucks! and there is an easy fix in my opinion. First thing to fix is the position of the cue in the back hand.  Should be able to hold and move the cue like a light saber and then once your lined up for the shot, then you cantrigger your table hand to come into play, which also is in the wrong orientation in the game at present!!Then the distance between cue hand and table hand needs to be lengthened and last once your set to take the shot you should be able to use your table hand controller joy stick to position where you hit the cue ball. Fix that and you will have a game that will start to feel like the real thing as at the moment it’s frustrating as hell and doesn’t resemble anything like what feels real. Certainly wouldn’t pay anything till this was fixed. Only giving it 2 stars and not 1 because it does have potential

i like the game play it's nice and smooth, it's a nice little game to pass the time if your waiting on something. it's a lot of fun i also like the setting that it's in ... kinda gives me a mafia style vibe. love the color aesthetic too, its a very homey feel to it.

This game is super fun! 🎱 I played it for about 15 minutes and didn’t want to stop. It really feels like you’re playing real pool, but in VR. The controls are easy to learn, and the game runs really smooth. The graphics look great!
It’s one of the best games I’ve tried in VR. If you have a Meta Quest, you should definitely check it out!

reviews.review_title: I Came for a Quick Game… 2 Hours Later…
reviews.review_description:
I was just gonna test it out, y’know, “5 minutes tops.” Fast forward two hours and I’m lining up trick shots like I’m in the VR Olympics. This game is dangerously fun.

The physics are so good I caught myself leaning on the virtual pool table—10/10 immersion, 0/10 for balance. Controls? Smooth like butter on a hot pancake. Graphics? Clean enough to make me forget I’m still in my living room wearing a headset and fuzzy socks.

I’ve played real pool and let me tell you, this version doesn’t require chalk or awkward small talk with strangers at the bar. If you’ve got a Meta Quest and like fun (who doesn’t?), just download it already. Your virtual cue is waiting.
Its alright....

I hadn’t played pool in years, used to shoot a few games with friends at the local bar, but life got busy. I picked up 8 Ball Pool on my Quest just to see what it was like, and honestly, I was surprised. Pretty solid! and once you get the hang of the controls, it really does feel like you’re playing on a real table. The 1920s-themed lounge adds a nice touch, making it feel like you’re back in a classic pool hall. It’s not perfect, but for someone who misses the feel of the game, this brings back a lot of good memories. Definitely worth a try if you’re into pool you will honestly never regret it 😀

I’ll be honest. I got this game because a) it was free, and b) I liked the controls. I played it for a while, beat all the ai opponents , then went on with my life. Sure, the YouTube player didn’t work properly (you can’t login so you are stuck with guest mode), and the ai was a bit bad, but it was fun, and free. I have miracle pool, and at the time the controls were rubbish - so I preferred this game to the one I actually paid for. 
Today I ran up the game to play for the first time in ages, and it kicked me out after a game, telling me I had to “buy” it. Ok then how much. I can part with a couple of pounds for it…. But wait, It’s a subscription now?? For HOW MUCH? Per MONTH?? 
So I deleted the game immediately. Shame really.  By the way, I fired up miracle pool - they fixed the controls. In that game There’s no subscription, one off cost, multi player if you like that sort of thing, better physics, more fun.only 2 down sides - no YouTube player, and I dont like the controls to move the table. I can live with that.
I play eight ball in real life and its calming for me
